_doc: Public conservative defaults for institution-safe mode. Local installs override via config/access-permissions.local.json (gitignored). See docs/INSTITUTION_SAFE_MODE.md for the full posture, docs/APPROVAL_TEMPLATE.md for private approval records, docs/FUTURE_FEATURE_REVIEW.md for the scope ledger.
_doc_institutionSafeMode: When true, policy denials throw PolicyDeniedError. When false, denials are logged but not enforced (telemetry-only). Public default is false so a fresh clone runs out-of-the-box; institutional installs flip this to true in their local override.

ai_providers._doc: Runtime-level allow/deny. Gates whether a runtime can spawn at all. Installing the SDK skill (/add-agentSDK-*) is necessary but not sufficient — the runtime must also be allowed here.

claude.rationale: Anthropic Claude SDK runs inside the container with a host-side credential proxy. No host-direct API calls.

codex.rationale: OpenAI Codex SDK runs inside the container. Credential mounted via per-group runtime home; no host-direct API calls.

gemini.rationale: Not currently approved for this install. Enable in local override only after institutional review.

local.rationale: Local model endpoint not configured. Enable in local override after confirming the endpoint runs without external egress.

host_ai_operations._doc: Narrow allowlist for direct host-to-AI calls. Anything not listed is implicit-deny. New host-side AI uses require an explicit entry plus institutional review.

email_residual_classifier.rationale: Residual email classification only — bucket 1 (action_templates) and bucket 2 (skip_senders) are resolved deterministically host-side. Only the residual reaches AI. Body cap, noise stripping, sort_folder taxonomy validation, and audit hash (body_sha256) are all enforced before any data leaves the host. See src/email-preclassifier.ts for the ADR block.

data_sources._doc: Operation-level allow/deny per data source. Operations narrower than what the granted scopes technically permit — e.g., Mail.ReadWrite scope is granted, but delete/send/destructive moves are denied at the operation layer.

ms365.rationale: Delegated personal-productivity workflow: own mailbox, calendar, and tasks. Drafts only — no autonomous send. No deletion. No shared mailbox. No app-only tenant-wide reads. Operation table is narrower than what the granted Mail.ReadWrite scope technically permits.

gws.rationale: Google Workspace at parity with MS365 for personal mail/calendar/tasks plus full Docs/Sheets/Slides authoring control. Drive list+read is allowed for finding referenced documents; arbitrary file uploads, sharing, and permission changes are denied.

imap.rationale: IMAP is a placeholder provider definition. Enable per-account via local override only after institutional review of the specific IMAP host and credential storage.

onedrive_sharepoint.rationale: OneDrive/SharePoint connectors are out of scope. Reading institutional file stores requires separate review.

google_drive_general.rationale: General Google Drive file connector is out of scope. The GWS provider above grants Docs/Sheets/Slides authoring plus list+read access for navigation; arbitrary Drive file operations require separate review.

teams_chat.rationale: Teams chat read/write is out of scope. Requires separate review with explicit Chat.* scopes; not granted by default.

lms_course_systems.rationale: Learning-management-system connectors (Canvas, Blackboard, etc.) are out of scope. Course/student records access requires separate institutional review.

obsidian_vault.rationale: Knowledge-extraction-to-Obsidian is a planned future feature. Enable per-vault in local override after confirming the vault contains no restricted data.

channels._doc: Channel-level allow/deny. Determines which inbound channels can wake or trigger the agent. Each entry should make explicit whether it is a self-only channel or a multi-user remote-control surface.

telegram.rationale: Personal bot binding to the user's own main group. Sender allowlist enforced upstream. Not a multi-user remote-control surface.

slack.rationale: Out of scope. Slack channel skill exists but is not enabled by default.

whatsapp.rationale: Out of scope. Personal WhatsApp bot skill exists but is not enabled by default.

emacs.rationale: Local Emacs HTTP bridge — out of scope by default; enable in local override if used.

http_api.rationale: Out of scope. If enabled, must bind to 127.0.0.1 only and require an API token.

gmail_inbound.rationale: Email-as-a-channel (inbound mail triggers the agent) is distinct from the GWS data-source provider. Out of scope by default.

nanovoice.rationale: iOS voice app channel — out of scope by default.

future_features_requiring_review: Shared mailbox or shared calendar access
OneDrive / SharePoint / Drive general file access
Reading course rosters or student records from any system
New AI vendor not in this registry
External plugins, bots, or remote-control channels not in this registry
Generating presentations or materials from institutional data sources
Per-content data classification (regex/ML scan within unstructured input)

private_approval._doc: Private approval records (approver, ticket, app ID, tenant ID, scope details) are stored in docs/APPROVAL.private.md and config/access-permissions.local.json — both gitignored. See docs/APPROVAL_TEMPLATE.md for the template.

logging._doc: Audit logging policy. The host-side preclassifier already records body_sha256 and body_chars_sent per residual classification (see src/email-preclassifier.ts). This section governs what the policy layer itself logs.
